titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Josephine Steiner Barger Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Matthew T. MacLean

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 09:34

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, clippings, photographs, and memorabilia documenting the personal and professional life of Josephine Steiner Barger.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of James D. Barger, 1997; M. Susan Barger, 2002-2003.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Josephine Steiner Barger Papers (MS 1729). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in two series and two additions: I. Topical Files, 1913-1984. II. Photographic Materials, 1912-1969.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Josephine Steiner was born in Bluffton, Ohio, in 1913 to Dr. Josiah and Augusta Steiner. Her father served in the medical branch of the Army in World War I. She was raised as a Mennonite and graduated from Bluffton High School and Bluffton College in 1930 and 1932, respectively. In 1934 Steiner earned her B.A. degree from Ohio University. She then attended Yale School of Nursing, where she received a Master of Nursing degree in 1938. When World War II began, Steiner joined the 39th General Hospital, a medical unit which was formed of Yale graduates. She served the war effort in New Zealand, Saipan, and Guadalcanal. From 1946 to 1949 Steiner was superintendent of nurses at Lake Maracaibo Hospital in Venezuela. She returned to the United States in 1949 and earned a Master of Science in Nursing degree from Western Reserve University in Cleveland, Ohio in 1951. Steiner later became superintendent of nurses and director of nursing at Good Samaritan Hospital in Phoenix, Arizona. In 1952 she married Dr. James D. Barger; thereafter they moved to Las Vegas, Nevada. The Bargers had two children, Michael Thomas and Mary Elizabeth, and also adopted James D., Jr. and Mary Susan. Barger died in 1971.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of correspondence, clippings, photographs, and memorabilia documenting the personal and professional life of Josephine Steiner Barger.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series is arranged in alphabetical order by topic, and consists of correspondence, clippings, and professional and personal documents.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous [Social Security card, bank account record 1955 planner, Bluffton News]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series is arranged by type. It consists of photographs, slides, and photograph albums recording the personal and professional life of Josephine Steiner Barger. The photographs section is arranged in alphabetical order by topic. The photograph albums and slides sections contain pictures of friends, vacations, her professional environment, and local life from the South Pacific in World War II and Venezuela from 1942 to 1949.

did.unittitle: Biographical and genealogical material regarding Josephine Steiner Barger, James Daniel Barger, and Josiah Steiner and family
Calling cards from Bluffton, Ohio High School, Class of 1930
Elementary school and high school photographs of Josephine Steiner Barger
World War II [39th General Hospital, New Zealand, and other views of the Pacific
Photographs, correspondence, and other miscellaneous papers relating to Lucia Allyn and Dr. Clarence Robbins.
39th General Hospital, List of Home Addresses of Male Officers
